title: ED discharge documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.
examination.rationale: The exact discharge-letter section supports recording measured triage data, investigations or procedures already performed, disposition, referral and handover only as clinician-confirmed facts.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.

exact_section.heading: GP Communication and example discharge-letter headings

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting the clinician-confirmed diagnosis or outcome and plan, patient understanding, specifically required follow-up and safety-net communication.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact patient-communication section supports documenting patient concerns or goals, the clinician-confirmed plan, completed risk discussion and specifically arranged follow-up; it does not decide discharge or generate advice.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting the clinician-confirmed diagnosis or outcome and plan, patient understanding, specifically required follow-up and safety-net communication.

sources_used.title: Discharge to General Practice
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency and primary care; requires UAE pathway adaptation

source_limitations: Diagnosis, treatment, medicine, pending results, responsible clinician, transport, destination, exact instructions, contact method and condition-specific safety net remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
